channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: There is no point investing in the stock market if you dont already have a lot of money Doing small buys into the market… — 55 replies — 220.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
There is no point investing in the stock market if you dont already have a lot of money Doing small buys into the market…

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-07-06 05:29 UTC

OP

There is no point investing in the stock market if you dont already have a lot of money

Doing small buys into the market in the hope you have a nest egg in your late 60's is retarded as fuck

>inbefore: YoU mUsT bE a ZoOomeR

Im almost 40. I built shit and made millions in 20s and 30s. It was only when I had over $5M liquid did I start putting my money in the markets.

You're all retarded as fuck for investing when you have like 50k to ur name, absolute massive cock fuck shit waste of time, go take that 50k and BUILD SOMETHING that makes you 500k to 5M in the next few years instead of waiting around like a fucking moron waiting 40 years for it to compound there to 500k.... because 500k in 40 years will be fucking piss shit dick nothing, you dumb faggot

bitch

pic related: me everytime i hear people telling people who are FUCKING POOR to put money in the markets "even just a lil bit counts" - NO IT FUCKING DOESNT LOL

Early replies (first 3)

62443219
>>62443208
elite s tier high iq post

62443220
>turning money into more money is bad
Okay zoomer go back to /r/communism

62443224
>I built shit and made millions in 20s and 30s. It was only when I had over $5M liquid did I start putting my money in the markets.
sure you did bro
this is just another vague "go make a business bro and make millions bro" post that is completely useless

The vast majority of people do not have the connections or the disposable cash to do anything but work and invest for decades

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445573
>>62445530
It is noticing people getting a leg up could be doing much better if they were as smart as me. Sad to see them squander it while I struggle to get to where they started out.

62445589
>>62445527
That would take 100s of years working while begging for constantly wage increases that always get shot down. Less of a hassle to invest.

62445594
>>62443208
trading crypto is unironically the only way to make it if you start small

62445602
>>62443208
I might as well put some money in the markets and get shit in order, it’s not meant to be plan A it’s meant to be a fallback plan if nothing else goes well.

>dude just build something worth millions
Easier said than done

62445625
>I built some bullshit SaaS company during a time of 0% interest rates and before the commoditization of all software with AI and you can too!
Fuck off millennial. You're just a boomer but 1000 times gayer

62445658
>>62445625
>I will put my $500 per month in the stock market and will have $5M in 50 years
>I'll finally be able to afford a 1 bedroom studio in the hood im gonna be the most chad 75 year old

62445661
Don't listen to boastful idiots. Just work hard and protect your money with your life.

62445664
>>62443208
Putting shit into the shit and piss 500 is still better than saving up. So wtf you talking about?

62445671
>>62443248
Or you could go to viewpoint214.com

62445728
>>62444037
But for it to compound you have to have a large volume of shares. And the average stock is too expensive to have a nominal amount to accumulate the compound effect...

Also the average stocks gains aren't typically that sizable. And tend to be volatile, so the other option is to invest in mutual funds and etfs. But those are extremely expensive and cost too much to start.

62445843
>just make 5 million dollars, simple

meanwhile 97% of millionaires come from either generational wealth, or slow steady contributions and compounding. The number of new millionaires from just housing equity holding 5 or 10 years is millions.

it's true that on a typical budget you'll never make a billion dollars. it's also true you can easily retire a multi millionaire in today's dollars for the price of a daily coffee habit.

if you want to be fit do what fit people do, eat clean and workout. if you want to be rich, do what rich people do, buy stocks and real estate. everyone wants a magic easy button, there isn't one, just buy a lotto ticket or your favorite shitmeme.

62445846
>>62445728
>But for it to compound you have to have a large volume of shares.
Nope. It will still compound at the same rate even with 1 share, as long as your fund supports fractional shares for DRIP.
>invest in mutual funds and etfs.
This is a very good idea for diversification, but does not matter for compounding.
>But those are extremely expensive
Only if you buy the ones that are expensive. While Vanguard's VOO is $684/share, Schwab's SWPPX is only $19. Both track the S&P 500 index. Vanguard supports fractional shares for DRIP. Schwab's SWPPX is cheap because Schwab doesn't like its funds to have a high purchase price so they do stock splits whenever the price gets too high (SWPPX recently had a 6:1 split), and it also has fractional shares for DRIP. Schwab's SCHG (large-cap growth ETF) is only $34/share (recently had a 4:1 stock split).

62445852
>>62445728
lack of a 7th grade math education, checks out. exponential is exponential, regardless of the base number.

exactly how does does say 10 compound slower than 1000? no you won't live off your dividends of 10 shares (or 1000) but they both compound at the same percent rate. thats why its more important to start today and be consistent than what start today means as a dollar figure.

62446567
>>62445846
That's not even close to true. It sounds believable though. You need a bunch of shares.

If you have ten shares and a fraction of a share that doesn't mean anything.

Unless you're in the s&p, but even then your rate of increase is significantly lower because the factor by which you compound or the whole way the compound effect is supposed to work or be most effective is by VOLUME.

10<100<1000

62446574
>>62445846
So you e got 34 thousand dollars to buy a thousand shares of it. So that if it increases you make a thousand times whatever it goes to. So if it goes to 100 dollars then you would make 66,000 dollars?

62446586
If you didn't have any money at all and wanted to make the best possible investment you could. Viewpoint214.com is still the best solution by far. And it's not even close.

62446799
>>62443208
You don't have any money, you're another
>Build a business
Shill
Fuck off

62446802
>>62443240
Okay retard what's your million dollar business idea.

62446804
>>62443248
>>in which case you are playing the market casino and can beat the casino
You don't need to "beat" the "casino" you fucking retard, you just need the market to not crash.

62447963
>>62443208
>put money in the markets "even just a lil bit counts"

the people that need to hear that are goycattle anyway and for them it would help. Sure they might not utilize even a few 100k but its the mentality that counts for the next generation, even if its a minority of people, its still wealth creation that would otherwise never happened. So given that if a goycattle baseline is to consume, pivoting from that is just good.

62448011
>>62443208
The whole idea that you need a hoard of money and that hoarding money is good is retarded anyways.

money is only good in circulation.

62448023
>>62443208
>Justd... Built something.
Didn't think of that. Thanks.

62448828
>>62443208
>There is no point investing in the stock market if you dont already have a lot of money

real

>go take that 50k and BUILD SOMETHING

build what you fucking nigger?

62448835
>>62448828
A business dumbass

62449473
>>62443227
You say this knowing full well that crony capitalism is the issue, not capitalism itself

62449567
>>62449473
Neither are a problem just excuses by dysgenic losers

62449871
>>62446567
If you invest $1000 and the stock price moves 5%, you are 5% richer regardless of whether you bought 1 $1000 share, ten $100 shares, or 100 $10 shares.

Although if you had bought the 100 shares, you could sell option contracts on it for extra money.

62449885
>>62449871
>sell puts
>stonk crashes and you are now bagholding
>sell calls
>stonk moons and you are now left with peanuts

62449911
>>62449885
Yep, why do brain dead poors think they can beat smart people?

62449972
>>62445728
Mutual funds and ETFs are always cheaper than buying into every stock yourself lol, what are you talking about? That's the entire point of them.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 109 replies — 158.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 109 • Updated: 2026-07-06 04:54 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442214
>>62426512
Buy the dip, obviously.

62442227
>>62439167
But that won't help the price up

62442228
>>62442214
Now???

62442255
>>62426512
I dca 60% of my savings each month while saving the remaining 40% for potential lower lows as we approach October, at which point I'll start going in with all of my monthly savings and that 40% I've been holding back.

62442503
>>62442227
Maybe not immediately. But without infrastructure, there's nothing to drive long-term value anyway.

62442507
>>62442214
Yup, little by little.

62442511
>>62442507
>>62442228
I dollar cost. THe past few months have been gr8

62442553
>>62442511
Where do you do that

62442563
>>62426512
I am DCAing in

62442650
>>62442503
memecoins pump faster. I made money on Ansem and some Solana gems

62442655
>>62439758
never trust centralised services

62442658
buy btc goys

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668
Goy

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998
>>62444608
really

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

62445689
>>62444998
DYOR

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

item.title: ITT: hard pills for /biz/ to swallow >your main wealth creation engine is your INCOME — 31 replies — 67.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT: hard pills for /biz/ to swallow >your main wealth creation engine is your INCOME

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-07-06 05:08 UTC

OP

ITT: hard pills for /biz/ to swallow
>your main wealth creation engine is your INCOME

Early replies (first 3)

62447104
>the most reliable way for you to have gotten wealthy was getting into an ivy league from highschool

62447109
>>62447101
Its actually how much cum you take in...swallow that

62447112
>>62447101
bullshit

there are only so many hours in a day and only so many of those hours that you can work, and only so much money you can get paid for each of those hours

People with an IQ above 110 realize this instinctively. They know working will NEVER make them rich. You have to find a way to get money for other people's work if you want to get wealthy. You need OTHER PEOPLE'S income to get rich.

the only way to do this is to start a business and get money from other people when they buy your stuff and money from other people when they work for you. There's a reason almost every wealthy person owns a business. It's literally the only way for most people to get rich.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447118
>>62447112
this
/thread

62447122
>>62447112

That counts as income still, I never said it had to be from a job. The point is that having low income and then focusing on trying to invest that low income really well in stocks or crypto or whatever it’s the path to financial success.

62447125
>>62447122

*isn’t that path to financial success

62447126
>>62447112
Your business's income would still be your INCOME would it not? You didn't actually dispute the point that finding ways to increase that is the primary goal...

62447132
>>62447122
>That counts as income still
I see you don't own a business.

all my "income" goes directly back into the business to make more money and so I don't have to pay taxes on it. I then borrow against the value of the business and pay myself just enough to service the loan.

My income is lower than almost anyone else here. I have far more "money" available than 99% of people though. And my "money" is busy making more money.

62447134
>>62447126
>Your business's income would still be your INCOME would it not?
Not according to the IRS or my bank.

>>62447122
I agree with your point regarding investing. Just differ on what counts as income I guess

62447136
You can do everything right and still fail.

62447143
>>62447136
It's normal to fail. Failures outnumber successes by a factor of ten. That's why you learn from your failures and keep trying.

Also success is temporary but I'll let /biz/ learn that bitter pill on their own. You need to succeed not just once, but many times over and over. There is not "Make It." Making it is an ongoing process for most people.

62447417
>>62447101
The best financial decision for a home owner to make is to sell, put everything into stocks and then rent. The gains will more than offset rent and you will get richer faster.

62447424
>>62447417
Loser strategy, you are trading a sure thing for a risky gamble

62447450
>>62447424
The stock market has had a 10% return annually on average for the last 100+ years. It's not exactly gambling. If at any point in history you sold the average home and put the money into stocks you'd make money in the long run. It's never not been true in history.

62447474
>>62447450
>average

Lmao you really are dumb

62447475
>>62447101
The Federal Reserve doesn't print money.

62447634
>>62447112
>almost every wealthy person owns a business
That's not true at all. Most wealthy persons (i.e. liquid net worth > $1m) are school teachers and engineers and accountants and doctors who invested as much of their income as they could manage. Business owners come in a distant 5th. Something like 90% of businesses fail, and of the ones that "succeed" well over 90% just kind of drift along making just enough for the owner to survive.

62447639
>>62447450
Oh to be this young and innocent again. Watch and weep, little one.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Yl3NxTS_DgY

62448879
>>62447634

A lot of the time owning a business is basically just owning a job (self employed).

62448952
>dude just increase your income, lmao

62448967
>>62447134
>Not according to the IRS or my bank.
you might want to read form 1040 a little more closely. hint: it's line 8.

62449006
>>62448952

If you live in a developed country, why not? What’s stopping you?

62449041
>>62447122
>I never said it had to be from a job
>babby pretends to not know the difference between implicit and explicit statements

62449119
>>62447104
This is like saying the most reliable way to get wealthy is to make a successful company. Few can do it.

62449155
>>62449119
that's the point you retard, that was your best chance.

62449166
>>62447101
My income is from flipping meme stocks on leverage

62449297
>>62447475
The Federal Reserve plus large banks and people and businesses borrowing money from the large banks, and the US government, all work together to print money.
It’s purposefully opaque and convoluted so that we don’t understand what’s happening or why, but to say that the Federal Reserve doesn’t print money may be technically accurate but they’re still part of the machinery that allows money creation.

62449336
>>62447634
over 90% of multimillionaires own businesses
>>62448967
idiot

62449411
Can you stop posting this nigger shit

62449481
>>62447634
$1M is not wealthy in the current year.

The millionaire next door trope only gets you 1 to 1.5M. To make real money you need to own a business or get in early to a business with equity compensation.

62449934
>>62447101
>just work for 40 years lmao
slavery will only keep you in slavery
bagging the fries will never get you a house
the regime is incredibly unlikely to improve your cost of living
they will keep printing forever, your boss will not proportionately raise your pay
act smart to stay ahead of the mob
can only be done by accelerating your income velocity
one life, its worth a shot
i hate the antichrist

item.title: Things no rich person said ever: >my key to success was racking up those CREDIT CARD POINTS — 29 replies — 54.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Things no rich person said ever: >my key to success was racking up those CREDIT CARD POINTS

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-07-06 05:03 UTC

OP

Things no rich person said ever:
>my key to success was racking up those CREDIT CARD POINTS

Early replies (first 3)

62446465
I had one on my last car that was a 94 Camry that looked like it was quadruple salvage (it was)
arguably funnier on that car but I feel as though the Prius also gets the point across

62446470
>>62446450
things no nigger said either is you need 1 BTC and be done.

62446478
>>62446465

Well done anon. I drive a 2014 RAV4 , Ramsey Approved

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62446490
>>62446478
funny enough, Ramsey actually despises Priuses because he says they aren't "manly"
in a way they kinda aren't though, just like all performative males feel like other men who can't grow a beard or eat the hottest hot sauces aren't "manly"

the Prius is paid off, low miles, fills up for $40, and hasn't broke down on me yet; also I only paid $450 last week for a whole new set of tires. I'm saving and investing all the money I would've been dumping into my old BMW for a new Tacoma in 1-2 years time; maybe a motorcycle and an R34 GT-R if the next bullrun is good

Prius daily scraper while the cool and fun vehicles stay nice and clean, only to be taken out for playtime :)

62446495
>>62446490
Sounds like the guy wants to keep his audience poor, good thing n o one takes him seriously

62446496
>>62446490

That’s why true car guys love the Honda Jazz/Fit

62446504
>>62446495
He makes losers seethe. Hes a national treasure and helps people get rich

62446510
>>62446504
he is the Boomer Caleb Hammer
he doesn't actually care about helping his clients or his audience; he only cares if he's entertaining and his audiences are such self-congratulatory boomers that they also only care about being entertained

you will learn more about finances and getting rich talking to your local Zillennial than you would ever learn watching hours of Ramsey

62446515
>>62446504
How do I know you're not poor though trying to hand me rotten advice

62446565
>>62446504

His methods are conservative but reliable. If you have high risk tolerance and discipline then maybe you can do better, but for 90% of people including most families etc following his advice will put you in a good financial position. Much better than your average normie.

62446577
99% of the population should just follow Dave's advice. He's right about credit card points because the majority of retards are paying high interest and think they're winning because muh travel points and cashback

62446583
>>62446490
I drive a Camry hybrid and it’s the best money I ever spent. I have to fill it up less than once a month. It’s a great car

62446587
>>62446450
>don't start a business, almost all of them fail in a few years

62446588
>>62446565
The litmus test for Dave Ramsey is do you think it's okay to buy or lease a brand new vehicle when you make less than 100k or do you think it's a good idea to take out student loan debt

62448653
>>62446587
Yes. He's quite right about this. You start a business after you've already "made it" and have enough spare cash laying around that you can afford the startup grind.

62448661
>>62446450
>the key to my success was never using credit cards because tv boomer told me so

62448664
>>62446450
holy shit this much copie. Admit you lost this debate and move on

62448818
>>62446450
The arguement is creditcard points pay for your flights you ignorant ass.

62448836
There is no reason not to pay for everything with your cc you have set to auto pay off every month and rack up points or a little cash back. If you are disciplined. Most are not is why this "boomer" advice is made for the retard masses such as he would not take a billion dollar loan at 0% interest.
The safety of a cc vs dc use alone if ever your # is stolen makes cc vastly superior.

62448841
>use credit cards to buy stuff I was going to buy anyway
>pay it off quickly
>get free shit for spending the money that I needed to use anyway
???

62448844
>>62448653

And when most normies think of starting a “business” they’re thinking of a restaurant or something that has an extremely high failure rate.

62448845
>>62448836
>>62448841

>you think you’re smarter than the bean counters at JP Morgan Chase?

62449428
>>62448818
Flights where? How many vacations are you talking a year? All you're doing is spending even more money there, how are you being "successful" by spending extra money, to get discount on traveling somewhere to spend more money?

62449786
>>62448845
That's one of my favorite Ramsey keks.
The first time I heard that, I thought
>yeah, Dave, I do think I'm smarter than the bank that issued me the card I use to get 5% back on groceries, since they needed a TARP bailout to avoid bankruptcy in 2008 (followed by a reverse split to avoid delisiting), and they've had other brushes with financial death, including one in 1991
I've never gone bankrupt (unlike Dave) or nearly gone bankrupt, like the bank.
Also 5% back on your groceries is huge, given how big an expense that is for most people.
I also have cards I use to get 5% back on utilities and cell phones.
Discouraging people from taking advantage of financial force multipliers like that, especially in this inflationary environment, is just irresponsible.

62449799
>>62448653
>a business
>just one
the average business owner has started multiple businesses because most of them fail. It's a numbers game. You don't start "a" business. You start as many as it takes to make it.

for some people this is one. For most it's closer to a dozen.

>>62448844
>they’re thinking of a restaurant or something that has an extremely high failure rate.
exactly. You have to run through the bad ideas before you can figure out the ones that actually work. Most people have no idea. People who succeed in business mostly work in construction, industry, or business services.

62449803
>>62449786

The banks have done the numbers and found that for every 1 person like you there’s 10 people who will use the card to rack up heaps of debt they can’t pay off and will pay huge interest every month. Dave is talking to the average person.

62449816
>>62449799

Also most people that start a successful business are 40-50 years old when they found it

62449927
>>62449803
Apparently it's more like half and half:
>>62449803
but maybe the typical brainlet Ramsey bot skews towards the half that pays interest.

62449930
>>62449927
Sorry, the link:
https://wvutoday.wvu.edu/stories/2025/03/17/wvu-research-shows-credit-card-behaviors-are-lifelong-whether-users-carry-debt-or-pay-balances-monthly

item.title: This has been the most tame BTC bear market so far What does it mean? — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
This has been the most tame BTC bear market so far What does it mean?

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-07-06 05:31 UTC

OP

This has been the most tame BTC bear market so far

What does it mean?

Early replies (first 3)

62449179
>>62449174
who says its overr??

62449184
>>62449179
Nobody. It's right in the graph.

62449187
>>62449174
We still have 2 years of bear bro

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449190
Newfags either got rekt in alts or learned to just hodl btc

62449194
>>62449174
>bull market was supposed to go to 230k and cheeto ruinedi t
>bear market goes to maybe 40k

62449198
>>62449174
32k october 2026

62449200
>>62449174
it means the chicken jockey isnt even out yet

62449230
>>62449174
It was a pretty tame bull too.
It's almost as if as the market matures It's not going to simply play pong on your faggot rainbow lines.

62449818
>>62449190
This, i am buying eth when btc went below 55k!

62449841
>>62449174
There's still nearly 6 months left of this year.

62449865
>>62449841
it will go lower nigger
expect 20k-30k nigger

62449892
>>62449865
shhh, let the baggies think they caught the bottom

62449976
>>62449174
most tame bull market too

item.title: Could always be worse — 71 replies — 47.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Could always be worse

Open thread • Replies: 71 • Updated: 2026-07-06 04:06 UTC

OP

You could be a German wagie.

>no more AC
>no more sick leave

These are the early signs of a collapsing country. Just wait until the food riots hit.

Early replies (first 3)

62438577
former Blackrock manager is chancellor now, imagine.

62438607
>>62438545
Do you have to send this shit to govt? What if the employer doesn’t give a shit is someone going to audit this?

62438687
>>62438607
If this is anything like Germany's neighbouring country that I live in then the doctor sends it to the government when he inputs it into the system, and then it goes to your employer automatically from there so he knows whether to honour it or not.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62441375
>>62441365
yet the gubberment and companies wonder why nobody wants to wörk anymore......!

62441387
>>62439638
>>62440561
Yeah this is the Moment german boomers retire (60s had really a lot of kids here) and all the brown experts who came here will pay them their pension. Its really a master plan. It will work beautifully? I know the economy did not grow since 7 years but any moment now it will turn around. Maybe more tax on energy and we can go.

62441401
>>62441375
Yeah im hoping this spirals out of control for them. It has the potential
1.extreme high taxes and extreme high energy costs with absurd regulation
2.production shuts down, real wages get lower fast and competent young people leave
3.government refuses to cut spending effectively and cut regulation (thats a given the sytem is too entrenched, has to increase taxes even more, and inflation increases aswell
4.first point intensifying

62441402
>>62441372
The truth is they're all foreign agents. Wasn't Merkel ex-Stasi or something like that? AfD isn't the answer because they're just more of the same wearing the mask of being something different.

62441424
>>62441401
I would tell you Ivan to go away but i know you’re exactly at the right place if you want to turn retards against their own country and continent.

62441427
>>62438545
Isn't germany's woes due to getting rid of their nuclear reactors which are the only source of reliable energy that isn't a scam?

62441433
>>62438545
Germans a generally insufferable and completely brainwashed regarding their glorious past. 12 years not a slave.

62441451
>>62441402
>everyone is in on it!
While mostly true there comes a point where there are enemy factions.
Otherwise we would already have a world gov.
Anglosphere still bows to england and so does russia, japan and china.

62441472
>>62441451
Yeah and AfD with it’s obvious russian backing is an enemy faction, no matter how much it claims to be against immigrants and wokeism.

62441478
>>62438545
but i am
and -40% ytd
and fat
and no gf

62441575
>>62441375
For decades we've had this system and everyone was happy. The problem is that the economy is failing because German is very reliant on cheap energy which we've chosen to avoid ever since the ukraine war. If we had pivoted hard into manufacturing renewables or simply having kids we'd be fine.

>>62441427
The reactors we had weren't very good. Nowadays everyone is pro-nuclear because they're thinking of reactor types that weren't even conceptualized 10 years ago. Our problems are that we were reliant on cheap energy which we didn't bother producing and that we have no kids.

62442429
>>62441424
>>62441472
Just fuck off you retard. My relatives even fought against Russia in ww2. I bet YOU are the one not German, or what's your religion I wonder? Let me guess "atheism with Jewish family"? See >>62438768
And don't forget the government is the one prioritizing foreign interests.

62442433
>>62441478
Oooofff at least it's Friday.

62442461
>>62438545
That’s why I let myself be fired, collected unemployment and fucked off. Retarded country full of retarded autism max robots.

62443834
>>62440944
It would be a shame if you coughed into your hands and touched as many public surfaces as possible on the way to the doctor's.

62443931
>>62438545

Both of these are inconceivable to the Australian mind.

62444279
>>62438687
My shithole works this way too, it's ok

62444281
>>62440631
kek

62445477
>>62438545

62445676
>>62441097
what the fuck lol

62445684
the australian painter was right

62446070
>>62445477
Typical kike, gets money thrown at by frens, kikes his way through every bundesracket.

62446094
>>62438770
This problem happens especially when they are kikes(unapologetically evil, subversive and feudal), Germans(stupid, not saying it spitefully, just low IQ outside 1 area per person and zero common sense which ends up in the regulatory clusterfuck Germany and their EU is, energiewende, starting and losing WW2, etc.) or in the case of Merz, stupid AND kike.

62446167
>>62442429
Amazing arguments. Are you seriously denying all the connections AfD has to Putin’s regime? It’s the same shit with alt-right all across europe.

62446771
>>62446167
altrightfags are unironically glazing putin

62446847
>>62446771
Putin is the one good world leader

62447049
>>62438770
Kosher post.

62449791
>>62446847
100RUB ($0,0000000001) have been deposited in your account.

62449805
Merkel destroyed Germany, merz is finishing it off he will start a war with Russia he knows he cant win, the goal being the extermination of all german men just like they did to Ukraine , buy long term puts on the German market they will 1000X

62449815
>>62438545
Kek this is cucked. It's not even the corporation demanding it which makes this even worse.
>you vill work more while the rapefugees live on gibs
Kek imagine living in Germany and having to prove you're ill.

I just remembered they now have rules to prevent you from leaving for a prolonged duration despite the draft not being manadatory lol, lmao even. Oh but only males btw, females exempt.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 203 replies — 46.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 203 • Updated: 2026-07-06 01:10 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427228
>>62425849
I concede. I'll look for a more productive venture but I won't let the thread or general die, it will be some time before value is struck, I will admit that. I can be tenacious if nothing else.

62428130
>>62425890
emperorBTC's telegram has a basic bitch tldr set of material to learn from. also has a youtube where he goes over stuff. that's where I started and I like that he focuses on the absolute basics, although he leans heavily towards confirmation w/ volume which others may disagree with. btc focused but btc/crypto is perhaps the most effective use of TA.
basically there's no need to get fancy and the chart is the final source of truth because what the price does > what people cope and/or seethe about
support/resistance levels and price action with risk management (defined r:r) = compound as you get gud
once you commit you'll realize "the catch" on people actually make money from trading is is simply how retarded the overwhelming majority of people are that they simply cannot manage a few simple tenets to click buttons and make money, you likely being one of them if you don't develop a process and risk too much on trades
gl anon

62428141
>>62428130
also perps have exploded so trading equities for crypto natives has been a pleasure to avoid tradfi gatekeeping bs
follow the right people on twatter with alerts set to on and it's like having multiple research firms giving alpha for free-- key word "right" accounts. hopefully you have the pattern recognition to figure out which are spewing paid channel larps and which are providing genuine insight
that's basically the whole game. if you can get into a group chat with other chads that's even better because a network is stronger than you alone, although this is def an arena where it ultimately comes down to you mastering yourself (you can't copy trade your way to wealth but you can learn what others do/how they enter/exit positions)
legend trade is a new perps platform that's gamifying this if you wanna start there instead of hyperliquid (front-end using hyperliquid)
Also bigtrout300 on twitter and join his tg as well he's pretty dope. There's a lot of great accounts posting good shit right now. Some really good posters fade in/out of cycles while the scammers/extractors are always abundant, but just use discretion

62428170
>pic related
>"...focus on the chart as these information is very likely to be priced in already"
the "TA is astrology for men" line is used to make fun of retards who take it too far and treat it like divination when in reality it's all stacking probabilities/confluence in your favor as best you can, and from the above example you can see relying on your feed for information will almost always be slower than the chart

62428488
Finally some good posts

62428503
>>62428141
Big trout doesn't post on Twitter and his TG hasn't had activity since May. Any other suggestions?

62428780
>>62428503
His tg is BigTroutTrade and he posts on twitter all the time wdym

62428797
>>62428780
BigTrout Trading on youtube as well

other X goats that have been killing it IMO recently:
@MoneyPrinter0x
and my personal favorite currently:
@Wild_Randomness

there's tons of alpha just follow those people, then see what they repost, then follow the people they interact with that provide research, and even or especially follow the chinese/korean/japanese accounts that post comprehensive semiconductor/other market effort posts since translation is seamless now
never been a better time to go full NEET

62429207
>>62428797
But you're still under $10k?

62429246
>>62378965
Anon Belgium is literally doing very well in the world cup, you should make more of these predictions

62431586
bump.

62431599
probably nothing
bio protocol desci ai BIO now cookin with open ai, nvidia, anthropic, claude news check twita

62432055
>>62373752
Hey
I just started out in this crypto thing and bought $450 worth of solana. How do I become cool and multiply it

62432088
>>62432055
Be like me and listen to people within a group of other retards that are pushing a token. The last one tanked a $200 investment -98% but then he re-deployed the same token and it yielded a 40x from where it was called.

62432306
>>62432088
pardon my incompetence but I didn't understand much of what you just wrote anon

62432350
>>62428797
I had bad experiences following twitter people and waiting for signals even those that seem smart. It can be interesting but you’ll be lost if they randomly stop posting, they’re not funds giving you alpha but people that should have better things to do than post if their full job is actually trading.

I do still like @donalt and @cryptocred their show technical roundup is a rare non clickbaity show that just focuses on TA but most people are inherently frauds on social media

62432360
>>62432055
I don’t think Solana is a good buy right now it’s barely holding support and likely going lower to like 40 if I remember correctly

>>62432088
If you have a group like that be a hero to the community and share it to us

62432373
>>62432360
>be a hero to the community and share it to us
see
>>62385756
I recently held an airdrop for /biz/ too

https://warosu.org/biz/thread/62424715

62434369
Bump, I'm getting an early weekend because of the country's birthday. Will devote time to this thread.

62435112
>>62432350
skill issue. donalt? lol
you gotta follow the small accounts anon

62436077
>>62435112
Why would you rely on random accounts calls and not build the skill yourself? You are just fucked if you only listen to others, and you have no way to know they aren’t just lucky for a while until they drag you down with themselves

>donalt
Yes he may be old and washed but you can still learn a lot from two traders that never shilled some memecoin. Except for ftx but I guess everyone had an ftx sponsorship for a while

62436265
>UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD
>200 REPLIES

LMAOOOOOOOOOOOOOO OH NO NO NO NO AAAAAAAHHHHHH

62437580
>>62436077
did you read my first post? i literally said not to just blindly copy trade
there's nothing wrong with using great traders as confluence

62440778
bamp.

62442413
Bump

62443230
Man i wanna move to SLC someday

62444221
>>62443230
Why

62446809
I’m going to college and bc I graduated mitary style I have someone else saving half and half, even if my shortcomings are just too coming for me to live my best life I get to shoot

62446811
>>62446809
I’m also t50

62449394
Bump

item.title: my brother-in-law is a literal genius, like 150+ IQ, and worked his ass off over the last 15 years or so to become a suc… — 12 replies — 40.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
my brother-in-law is a literal genius, like 150+ IQ, and worked his ass off over the last 15 years or so to become a suc…

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-07-06 05:17 UTC

OP

my brother-in-law is a literal genius, like 150+ IQ, and worked his ass off over the last 15 years or so to become a successful entrepreneur and just completely failed. he's flat broke now. it's crazy to me how hard it is to make good money even if you're smart and hardworking. the world is fucking brutal.

Early replies (first 3)

62449875
>>62449873
“Smart” and didn’t go all in crypto. I shiggy diggy

62449876
Almost like being a business owner is just being a wagie with more work and all of your capital at risk.

62449881
>>62449876
Nah that's being self employed, an entrepreneur convertes other peoples time and labour into money

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449886
>>62449873
How did he fail? What was his niche? Do you know if he exposed himself to lumpy amounts of risk? I.e. a trade being able to lose him like 80% of his entire wealth.

62449887
High IQ people are exceptional at recognizing obscure patterns but somehow always manage to miss or disregard the most obvious ones. That's why they inevitably fail in a world run by sub 90 IQ average. High IQ is a curse, not a blessing, especially when it is not coupled with complete lack of empathy.

62449898
>>62449887
high iq people tend to overthink though, they'll project/forecast into the future (optimizing for the best yield, so delayed gratification) and if it's actually grim they'll fall into a depression because they realize the circumstances, but it's like knowing a nuke is going to go off in 10 days and you have 0 incentive to tell others because the incentive structure for anything financial means you should keep your edge by keeping intelligence (the other kind) confidential to yourself and the people you trust. no this isn't like "oh I know this stock will heckin go up", but rather shit about affordability and the realistic odds of whether you can get something or not and it's worth trying. so if you're too aware, you can't ride on hopium; maybe ironically purposely not think about it and try anyway because nobody can actually predict the future. but you can certainly sense where the tide is going, if you have an iron will then it can be served well but people dont get that maturity without a lot of pain and years.

62449909
More white incel loser cope

>waaaah whatever i could do is too hard
>waaah what if i dont instantly succeed
>waaah i deserve everything with no effort
>waaaah boomers got everything easy waaaaaah

62449923
>>62449873
Its probably because he's not that smart.

62449926
>>62449909
this is also true. relying on extrinsic outcome motivation is a 100% guaranteed way to an perma incel doomloop with the end-stage being suicide

62449942
>>62449909
Name one successful poojeet franchise

62449948
>>62449942
This is more white incel loser cope

>waaaah anyone who calls out my crybaby behavior is an Indian, a foid or a joo

Grow up

62449954
>>62449873
If he gives up after one failure he's not going to make it through the next 5 failures that will eventually teach him how to succeed

also geniuses don't usually do great at business. The sweet spot for starting a business is in the 100-120 range. Smart enough to make good decisions but dumb enough not to give up when things go wrong anyways.

OP

https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449480
Just, and I mean actually pause what you’re doing and imagine for a second, ponder the aroma

62449495
>>62449388
costco need to cancel their membership and just ban them

62449515
>>62449441
+1200 izzat for you my friend, to claim please get google gift card and give me code. Do not redeem.

62449517
>>62449400

62449539
>>62449441
Saaaaars

62449616
These are the niggas trying to shill you GRRR stock in /smg

62449623
>>62449539
Will white incels ever fight back or do they like the humiliation of giving away their country?

62449626
>>62449388
I'll post the same reply that I posted in your other thread on the exact same topic:
>>>/pol/538424408
>The sad thing is that a lot of people have done this, not just the poos. It's almost routine to see someone bringing back a totally worn-out backyard playset that has clearly spent years being torn up by their kids, or furniture that fell apart after years of use. The one thing that isn't really an abuse although it seems like it is when people bring back dead plants from their landscaping, because failure for the plant to take root and grow is considered a valid reason for return.

62449694
Jugaaad

62449716
>>62449441
>>62449623
Brown scammers like you need to go back to POOdia.

62449769
>>62449716
Make me

62449770
i would take my old furniture to costco for no other purpose than creating a physical obstacle for boomers trying to shop

62449829
>>62449441
Bloody brown bitch yes

62449846
>>62449769

62449850
>>62449770
But you dont. Why? are you lazy or a coward or just a blowhard incel?

62449862
>>62449388
Izzat status?

62449895
>>62449850
I'm a lazy coward blowhard incel but at least I'm making decent money from my investments.

62445016

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62445048
>>62444969
55k next weeek!

62445049
bobina is thick asf

62448136
Comforting sex with Bobina

62448224
>>62444969
i unironically think it is too big to fail
>if it crashes the US economy is fucked
>elites are depending on it for their mass datacenter surveillance state
>if US companies fail then chyna wins
US government will prop it up at all costs
prove me wrong

62448245
>>62444969
Unironically soon.

62448465
>>62444969
when you FOMO in

62448519
bobina dev is a deranged individual, stay away at all costs

62449610
>>62444969
IDK enough about the finances of the companies to say, but the demand for the hardware isn't going away any time soon. If you need hardware, buy now. When M5 studio drops, it's probably going to be up to 128gb of ram, and those will sell out in a heartbeat, further driving up prices on RTX 6000's and DGX sparks when people panic-buy whatever significant compute they can.

62449618
>>62444969
that's hard to predict. in the end, Ed Zitron will be proven right.

62449622
>>62444969
Bobina best grill

62449659
>>62448519
hello chudcel

62449668
Bobos in shambles

62449893
>>62444969

item.title: whats it like living in london? Chelsea looks unreal god damn if i was a billionaire i would live in london forever. Eve… — 34 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
whats it like living in london? Chelsea looks unreal god damn if i was a billionaire i would live in london forever. Eve…

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-07-06 03:53 UTC

OP

whats it like living in london? Chelsea looks unreal god damn if i was a billionaire i would live in london forever. Even if i had a few mill i would probably move there from USA.

Early replies (first 3)

62440816
Don't think you would, mate

62440835
>>62440727
Brilliant, best city in the world

62440843
>>62440727
lel london.

Moved away from Fulham and London 5 years ago thank fucking christ. Hell on earth.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62440873
>>62440727
its literal shit i genuinely dont understand what you find appealing about it ?

62440877
Check the demographic figures for London then come back here and tell us you still want to live there.

62440886
>>62440727
Not nice

62440968
>>62440727
Slave mind. But its good that you retards stay in the worst places in the world and leave thai beaches empty.

62440971
>>62440865
Mumbai and London is there a difference?

62440974
>>62440968
Thailand lmao how fucking depressing my god

62441031
>>62440974
>says the guy wanting to live in one of the worst cities in the world
>yeah pic related is depressing
>but concrete and a mix of jeets, niggers, moslems and assholes is good

62441040
A thread full of jeets seething because they don't live in London

62441046
>>62440727
>>62440816
>>62440835
>>62440865
>>62440873
Go back home. Nobody wants you to be in Europe.

62441083
>>62440727
Pretty sure it's like living in Pyongyang but browner

62441105
The trick is not to live in London but in the nice suburbs of the borders/surrounding home counties and enter London only for work and selected leisure

62441183
>>62441031
>he won’t ever respond to this

62444114
>>62441183
Yeah, it seems he left

62444152
Is this a troll post? What's so "unreal" about Chelsea?
Even if hustle and bustle is what you're after I'm pretty sure large American cities have more to offer given the higher purchasing power. 20% of Londoners are on unemployment benefits and then there's a large chunk of Londoners on full time employment who are too poor to afford anything and are also on benefits.
Add in all the illegal immigrants and non whites living under the radar and you get a city of 8 million people but the entertainment and night out options are there to cater for a city of 3 million or less because they're the only ones able to afford anything. When factor those things in London is about as vibrant as some mid-tier American city like Denver or Phoenix.

It's a bit like jeets bragging about the massive size of Mumbai not taking into account the fact Minneapolis has more people who are able to eat out once a week.

62444383
>>62440971
Mumbai has white people

62444388
>>62444152
I think I heard something like welfare obligations exceed tax receipts in the UK also, whole country is basically fucked

62444392
The UK is as poor as Mississippi, literally.

https://www.theatlantic.com/magazine/2026/07/uk-productivity-economy-reform-party/687303/

62445003
>>62440727
It's okay if you don't have to pay a mortgage or rent. The problem is it's overcrowded by the wrong people. Immigration from India is out of control and they are wrecking the place and turning it into a dump. Add to that there are Somalians everywhere and they contribute absolutely nothing. Their kids are animals. Then you've got the usual afro-black population growing (contribute nothing) but crime.

That said I've noticed an increasingly growing population of American elders here. They find the UK more peaceful and united than America apparently.

If you have a good job and earn above £45-50k, you can do ok, but not live expensively.

62447421
>>62440727
ANOBODY IS BUYING YOUR CHELSEA REAL ESTATE HAHAHHAHAHAHAHAHHAHHHAHAHAHAH

62447431
>>62441046
>britcuckstan
>europe

62447436
>>62440727
You're an incel innit?

62447551
London? England isn't even a real country anymore, if youre a lazy brown scammer it's probably the best place on earth

62447566
I lived there before Brexit and slept with many women from Eastern Europe that just arrived.

It was a great time to be in London.

I hear it's a crime filled shithole now.

62448147
>>62447436
what does that have to do with anything i jut really like london its a charming city.

Yes i am incel

62448178
>>62448147
That explains why. Also, why does it matter where you live if you never leave your house?
Live in Thailand and grow up.

62448193
>>62448178
how does that explain it?? london is for incels? huh i thought it was rich chads and women city is the migrant crisis that bad

62448252
>>62440727
Why on earth, anon? What makes you think London is a good place to live? NYC, LA, Miami are shitholes but I could understand if you picked places like that. See >>62444152

62448288
>>62448193
London is too social and cool for an incel like yourself gov, you're better off somewhere like Bangkok that'll pull you out of your shell innit

62448326
>>62448193
ur more likely to see a white person in middle of Cairo than London

62449797
>>62448288
thailand looks unbelievably depressing ngl the 40 year old divorced dudes clubbing with lady boys is brutal to watch.. ill just roam the streets of london

item.title: AI is dying — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI is dying

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-07-06 05:35 UTC

OP

>Over the weekend, I got a message from a tech company CEO outlining his TOTAL BAN ON AI across his organization. There are no exclusions. It is not limited to generative AI. It is not temporary. It is a full stop.
>It turns out that the corporate dream of a single “100x” human managing hordes of AI agents isn’t the optimal way to run a business

Sorry not sorry, you've been warned multiple times but kept throwing money into casino chips aka AI stocks.

https://www.inc.com/joe-procopio/the-first-company-wide-ai-ban-just-hit-my-inbox-heres-what-it-means/91357535

Early replies (first 3)

62449971
>The Ban on AI Research
>The CEO’s company had been dealing with a slump in conversion rates for the better part of 2026
>Turns out, AI-assisted research had resulted in casting a much wider net for prospects and dragged in many that were not nearly a fit. In some cases, the companies were defunct, and in one case, the company didn’t exist at all. The best the CEO could figure is that AI had just inferred it into existence

62449974
>The Ban on AI Agents
>One of the agents broke, in that was unable to scrape data it needed to generate a report. Its solution was to just generate the report anyway and then to freak out about the sudden change in what that report was saying, now that it was missing foundational information but treating it as if that entire business line had suddenly gone to zero.
>“It took a week to figure it out, because the guy who created the agent wasn’t the guy responsible for the report, and he was stalling while re-running months’ worth of reports by hand.”

62449977
>The Ban on Vibe Coding
>The reporting failure was the trigger for the ban.
>“When I discovered we had two different versions of the same module in our platform, connected to two different databases filled with two different sets of data stored in two completely different and unrelated [schemas], the next day I put the ban in place.”

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449981
>>62449963
I'm happy for you, or sorry that happened to you.

item.title: The Creampig Painpoint — 7 replies — 18.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Creampig Painpoint

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-07-06 05:12 UTC

OP

I'm trying to think of business models that will solve a simple set of painpoints: horny young men and poor young women. Basically, there are millions of young men with money who want to cream in a slampig (a creampig, if you will), and there are also millions of slampigs with no money, but all the obvious ways of letting young men pay for creampigs fall under prostitution, so the business model doesn't work in 90% of jurisdictions. Is there any way to make this work that ISN'T prostitution?

Early replies (first 3)

62449866
>>62449842
We get it anon. You like fatties. I do too. Not everybody wants to pay money to fill a hole.

62449884
>>62449842
It's called Tinder, and you're unfortunately late to the party on that one.

62449889
>>62449866
A lot of people do, though. Look at how big OnlyFans is, and that's all just cockteasing.

>>62449884
False. Tinder solved the painpoint of horny women who need to get fucked. Only a tiny percentage of men got anything out of it. It doesn't solve the horny men problem or the poor slampig problem.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449905
>>62449884
>and you're unfortunately late to the party on that one.
man you have no idea what you're talking about. what OP is talking about is curating a dating market that looksmatches and confines competition to a more equal standing or at least something more compatible. Tinder and every dating app has no incentive to do that, what they want to do and will always do until the end of time is enforce hypergamous and a competitive dating market but not excluding anyone and making them think they can score some random guy/girl that shows themselves in a picture and the "ooo maybe they'll heckin like me"; there's already fatigue and dating apps are best reserved for data mining or winners in hypergamy

62449916
>>62449842

Make a dating site and call it poorslampigs.com - problem solved

62449932
>>62449905
Yes, well said. Maybe the trick is to have AI do a weighted looksmatch (women are given a slight advantage because they're pickier) and just assign each person three and only three options per week. Women are paid to use the site; men are not. If the woman doesn't pick an option, she doesn't get paid.

62449943
>>62449916
You know what? I like it, unironically. Very direct.

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-07-06 05:35 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Extra Cash Besides a JOB — 17 replies — 6.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Extra Cash Besides a JOB

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-07-06 02:48 UTC

OP

Getting the obvious out of the way, everyone on this board is employed or in massive leveraged debt
That aside, what are you tried and true, consistent, easily accessible ways to make money on the side. Whether it's 50 bucks a week or 500 I don't care, just share some examples and provide actual explanations / results of the side gig whenever possible.
We are all on the same money making team bros let's help each other out

Early replies (first 3)

62446864
>>62446849
I fuck around on oil futures. Make like $25/day on average. It's time consuming but I do it during downtime at the office so why not.

62446865
Start a business and work at it 3 years you will be millionaire

62446869
>>62446865
what part of side or extra do you not understand dumb retarded faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62446903
>>62446869
he's right though
most side-gigs are just bad businesses

if you get a successful side-gig you're running a good business.

anyways my side gig was crime scene cleanup, hazardous waste cleanup, and office cleaning. I made millions doing it and now it's my main business.

62446919
>>62446869
You are missing the part where stop crying on incel threads and start a business

62446925
Donate plasma, bonus it filters out microplastics; downside it takes two fucking hours.

62446939
i started a massage business and i only take clients if they sound hot...so far ive been able to make a good call 51% of the time every time.

62446991
>>62446903
See now im imagining dave ramsey telling people to buy a power washer and clean up crime scenes
anyway why is a multimillionaire on 4chan cmon

62446993
>>62446991
To show you losers the way. What you think millionaires don't shit!

62446997
>>62446991
in my generation millionaire isn't anything special. The median net of people my age is almost half a million, meaning at least a third of us are millionaires. Mostly because we bought houses way back in the late 1900's

anyways crime scene cleanup is pretty specialized. It's not regular work for most people because there just aren't that many murders and suicides. But it pays hundreds of dollars per hour so it's worth doing if you can.

62447037
Do you have any of those stores that buy and sell returned Amazon goods near you? Go on a restock day with a barcode scanner app handy and pick up any fancy electronics. It's pretty easy to find 50$+ things like headphones, watches, and more niche devices to resell on marketplace, eBay, etc.

62447512
>>62446849
unironically buy pokemon sealed products and sell in 1-2 years for 3-5x

62447559
>>62447512
Pist a picture of the ones you bought for this opportunity

62447924
anyone know of good AI strats

62449474
Bump

62449570
>>62446991

62449661
>>62447924
Lots. Finding the energy to do it after doing similar shit all day at a full time job is a different story.

Thinking about just running a POD node on my 5090 to rent out inference, but no idea what the returns on it would be, just know that POD's been climbing.
